_doc: Weight manifest (R160). The single list of 'what the app expects on disk and where to get it'. Weights NEVER live in git; bulk weights sit in the parent monorepo's src/village_ai/models/ (outside this repo). Entry types: 'hf_diffusers' = pulled via diffusers from_pretrained into the HF hub cache; 'file' = a single weight fetched from the companion HF repo, checksum-verified. Presence-check -> offer fetch -> fail only if that fails (R163). Only models needed by the ACTIVE phase are fetched.
_companion_repo_status: PLACEHOLDER — companion HF repo not yet created. Update URL on creation; 'file' entry urls below resolve against it.

essential_for: P0 smoke target — zimage t2i batch (§12)
models.note: Public, Apache-2.0 diffusers repo pulled by stage1_load_pipeline.from_pretrained; integrity via hub etags. NOT mirrored in the companion repo.

essential_for: Muse generative SLM/VLM — all generative roles (R144)
models.note: Already present locally. sha256 TODO: fill at companion-repo publish.
Already present locally. sha256 TODO: fill at companion-repo publish.

_multi_presets_doc: Preset-aware weight sets for the `multi` casting pipeline (P1/M2, §5). DELIBERATELY NOT in the phase-scoped `models` list above: these are checked at /generate time for the SELECTED ideation preset only (POST /generate pipeline:multi). Folding them into the phase gate would force ALL presets' gated checkpoints onto every rig before any cast (over-gating a 16 GB target) — instead a cast fails fast (412) listing exactly the missing repos for that preset. Some are HF-GATED (need license acceptance on huggingface.co + an HF_TOKEN). Presence = a cache probe of `probe` under the platform-resolved repo (try_to_load_from_cache). Mirror of pipeline/multi IDEATION_PRESETS — keep in sync if that map changes. NOTE: the flux2 Klein text encoder is Qwen3 (variant 4B for klein-4b, 8B for klein-9b), NOT Mistral (only flux.2-dev uses Mistral, and the presets load Klein checkpoints + the FLUX.2-dev *AE* only). The flux2 loader uses the FP8 Qwen3 on CUDA but falls back to the non-FP8 repo on Windows ROCm (the RX 9070 XT target — flux2/stage1_load_models._load_text_encoder_safe). So the TE entry carries `repo_id` (non-FP8, used on Windows ROCm) + `fp8_repo_id` (used elsewhere); the gate probes whichever EXACTLY matches this platform (components._needs_fp8_workaround mirrors the loader's test).
_postproc_doc: Tool-scoped weight sets for the postproc-class adapters (P1/M3.5+). DELIBERATELY NOT in the phase-scoped `models` list (same rationale as multi_presets): folding a postproc weight into `weights_ok` would gate ALL of /generate on a tool the run may never use. Instead the tool's endpoint (e.g. POST /assets/{id}/stage-b/matte) checks ITS OWN set at request time and fails fast (412) with a fetch hint. Probe = a representative cached file; BiRefNet is a *transformers* repo (trust_remote_code, custom arch in-repo), so the probe is config.json — it has no model_index.json.

birefnet.note: MIT; general 1024 subject matting (~0.9 GB) — the default variant
MIT; high-res 2048 variant (~0.9 GB). Entry ids mirror the catalog variant ids — the matte endpoint's gate is VARIANT-aware (checks only the chosen one)

identity.note: ⚠ InsightFace RESEARCH/NON-COMMERCIAL (HF mirror, ~530 MB) — the M4 identity-lock swapper (spike: anchor cos 0.105→0.870). `filename` = single-file fetch (never an unrestricted snapshot)

InsightFace buffalo_l detection/recognition pack (~280 MB; research/non-commercial) — NOT an HF repo (github-release zip): presence is a FILESYSTEM probe at $LOOM_INSIGHTFACE_ROOT (default <HF_HOME>/insightface)/models/buffalo_l/<probe>; fetch hydrates via the insightface auto-downloader (M4 review: a fresh/offline rig must fail at the 412 gate with a fetch hint, never mid-job)

face_restore.note: GFPGAN v1.4 as ONNX (~340 MB; upstream GFPGAN is Apache-2.0; facefusion model mirror) — the M6 restore pass. ⚙ Deliberately ONNX, not the gfpgan/basicsr pip stack (breaks on modern torchvision; M6 spike: ~0.3 s/face CPU). ⚠ `filename` is REQUIRED here: the mirror repo hosts dozens of models — an unrestricted snapshot would pull them all (M6 review)
shared detection/alignment pack (see the identity entry) — listed here too so THIS tool's gate is self-contained
